cf_parsed.subtask_body:
I tightened the optimality check around the only possible way a cheaper objective than 77 could exist.
To beat 77 with 2+ frequencies, edge cost must be <47. But 47 is already the minimum Steiner tree edge cost:
- Cheapest connection between Port and Rock is direct Port-Rock = 22.
- Cheapest way to attach Pine to a connected Port/Rock-side network is via Cape-Rock-Pine = 17+8 = 25.
- Hence any tree spanning Port, Pine, Rock has edge cost at least 22+25 = 47, attained by Port-Rock, Rock-Cape, Cape-Pine.
Could a 1-frequency tree beat 77? Then edge cost would need to be <=61 and the active set must contain no interference pair.
- Any tree containing Port and Pine without Cape-Pine must connect Pine via Cliff, Dune, or Mill.
- Via Cliff requires Cliff-Pine 9 plus connection of Cliff to {Port,Rock}; cheapest is Cliff-Rock 25, so Port/Rock/Pine tree cost at least 22+34? More directly best such trees are Port-Rock + Rock-Cliff + Cliff-Pine = 56, but active set has Cliff-Port interference, so not 1-frequency.
- Via Dune gives best baseline-type Port-Dune-Pine plus Rock attachment, total 54, but active set has Dune-Port interference.
- Via Mill requires Mill-Pine 25 and Mill-Port 9, then Rock attachment at least 22 through Port-Rock, total 56+, and no cheaper route.
- Via Cape gives edge-minimal 47 but active set has Cape-Pine interference.
Thus every tree with edge cost <=61 has at least one interference edge, forcing at least 2 frequencies.
So objective lower bound is 47 + 30 = 77, matched by the current solution.
cf_parsed.updated_plan_state: Best found and now well-justified optimum is the 3-edge tree Port-Rock-Cape-Pine with 2 frequencies, total cost 77. No further search is likely to improve because edge-cost and 1-frequency lower bounds both match this solution.

parsed.subtask_body:
Enumerated cheapest tree ways to connect required Port, Pine, Rock.
A tree with only required nodes costs at least Port-Rock 22 plus one path from Pine to {Port,Rock}; cheapest is Pine-Cape-Rock = 25, total 47.
Using Steiner nodes can do better. Cheapest way to connect Pine is via Cape (8 to Pine, then Cape to Rock 17 or to Cliff 10 then Rock 25) or via Dune/Mill (both 25 direct), so best Pine attachment into a Port/Rock-side network is through Cape-Rock for 25 total from Pine to Rock.
Then connect Port to Rock as cheaply as possible: direct Port-Rock is 22, but Port-Bay-Rock is 24, Port-Dune-Rock is 27, Port-Mill-Bay-Rock is 40, etc. So with Pine attached through Rock, total 25+22=47.
Alternative is to connect Port to Cape/Pine side first: Port-Bay-Dune-Mill...? no cheap route to Pine under 25. Best Port to Pine path is Port-Bay-Dune-Mill? impossible; Port-Dune-Pine = 32, Port-Mill-Pine = 34, Port-Rock-Cape-Pine = 47. Then add Rock at least 17 more, giving 49+.
Promising explicit trees checked:
1) Port-Rock, Rock-Cape, Cape-Pine: edge cost 47.
2) Port-Bay, Bay-Rock, Rock-Cape, Cape-Pine: 49.
3) Port-Dune, Dune-Rock, Rock-Cape, Cape-Pine: 52.
4) Port-Bay, Bay-Dune, Dune-Pine, Port-Rock: 56.
5) Baseline Port-Rock, Port-Dune, Dune-Pine: 54.
Thus minimum edge cost appears 47, attained by tree Port-Rock-Cape-Pine.
Frequency minimization on active set {Port,Rock,Cape,Pine}: interference edges are only Cape-Pine. No other listed pair among these. So 1 frequency impossible, 2 frequencies sufficient.
Assign Port=1, Rock=1, Cape=1, Pine=2 (or swap Cape/Pine colors). Total = 47 + 15*2 = 77.
Lower bound for objective: need at least 2 frequencies for any tree containing Pine? Not always, but for the 47-edge optimum yes. To beat 77 with 2 freqs would need edge cost <47, impossible by enumeration/lower-bound above. With 1 freq would need edge cost <62 and no active interference; but any tree including Pine and connecting to others without Cape/Cliff? cheapest such is baseline-type 54 and includes Dune-Port interference, so not 1-freq. Candidate 1-freq trees are clearly much more expensive than 62. So 77 is very likely optimal.
parsed.updated_plan_state: Best found tree is Port-Rock, Rock-Cape, Cape-Pine with total 77 using 2 frequencies. Remaining value from more search is low; only residual check would be a formal proof that no 1-frequency tree under edge cost 62 exists, but current case analysis already makes optimality very likely.

transcript.content:
PLAN_STATE: Enumerate low-cost Steiner trees connecting Port, Pine, Rock by considering useful Steiner nodes Bay, Dune, Mill, Cliff, Cape. For each promising tree, minimize frequency count under interference constraints, noting 1 frequency works iff no active interference edge and otherwise likely 2 unless a triangle/odd cycle appears. Compare total = cable sum + 15*k. Use graph structure to prove or strongly justify optimality, not just find one candidate. Focus on cheap routes via Bay/Dune/Mill/Cape rather than expensive direct links to Pine/Rock. Keep final answer as valid tree plus frequency map only on active villages.
NEXT_SUB: {"id": 1, "desc": "Enumerate and compare the cheapest plausible Steiner trees connecting Port, Pine, Rock, then compute minimum frequencies for each and identify the best feasible solution with an optimality argument", "p_solve": 0.83, "time_budget_s": 240}

SUB_1: Enumerated cheapest tree ways to connect required Port, Pine, Rock.
A tree with only required nodes costs at least Port-Rock 22 plus one path from Pine to {Port,Rock}; cheapest is Pine-Cape-Rock = 25, total 47.
Using Steiner nodes can do better. Cheapest way to connect Pine is via Cape (8 to Pine, then Cape to Rock 17 or to Cliff 10 then Rock 25) or via Dune/Mill (both 25 direct), so best Pine attachment into a Port/Rock-side network is through Cape-Rock for 25 total from Pine to Rock.
Then connect Port to Rock as cheaply as possible: direct Port-Rock is 22, but Port-Bay-Rock is 24, Port-Dune-Rock is 27, Port-Mill-Bay-Rock is 40, etc. So with Pine attached through Rock, total 25+22=47.
Alternative is to connect Port to Cape/Pine side first: Port-Bay-Dune-Mill...? no cheap route to Pine under 25. Best Port to Pine path is Port-Bay-Dune-Mill? impossible; Port-Dune-Pine = 32, Port-Mill-Pine = 34, Port-Rock-Cape-Pine = 47. Then add Rock at least 17 more, giving 49+.
Promising explicit trees checked:
1) Port-Rock, Rock-Cape, Cape-Pine: edge cost 47.
2) Port-Bay, Bay-Rock, Rock-Cape, Cape-Pine: 49.
3) Port-Dune, Dune-Rock, Rock-Cape, Cape-Pine: 52.
4) Port-Bay, Bay-Dune, Dune-Pine, Port-Rock: 56.
5) Baseline Port-Rock, Port-Dune, Dune-Pine: 54.
Thus minimum edge cost appears 47, attained by tree Port-Rock-Cape-Pine.
Frequency minimization on active set {Port,Rock,Cape,Pine}: interference edges are only Cape-Pine. No other listed pair among these. So 1 frequency impossible, 2 frequencies sufficient.
Assign Port=1, Rock=1, Cape=1, Pine=2 (or swap Cape/Pine colors). Total = 47 + 15*2 = 77.
Lower bound for objective: need at least 2 frequencies for any tree containing Pine? Not always, but for the 47-edge optimum yes. To beat 77 with 2 freqs would need edge cost <47, impossible by enumeration/lower-bound above. With 1 freq would need edge cost <62 and no active interference; but any tree including Pine and connecting to others without Cape/Cliff? cheapest such is baseline-type 54 and includes Dune-Port interference, so not 1-freq. Candidate 1-freq trees are clearly much more expensive than 62. So 77 is very likely optimal.

Remaining value from more search is low; only residual check would be a formal proof that no 1-frequency tree under edge cost 62 exists, but current case analysis already makes optimality very likely.

DECISION: stop
